title: inula decurrens
intro: Inula decurrens, also known as the narrow-leaved fleabane or creeping elecampane, is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and creeping stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Inula decurrens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu. Inula decurrens can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
